title: Views about AI's impact on society in the next 20 years
subtitle: Survey respondents were asked, "Will artificial intelligence help or harm people in the next 20 years?"
caption:
Views about AI's impact on society in the next 20 years, 2021
Survey respondents were asked, "Will artificial intelligence help or harm people in the next 20 years?"

The stacked horizontal bar chart displays survey responses from 2021 regarding whether artificial intelligence will mostly help, mostly harm, or have a neutral impact on society over the next two decades. The data covers eleven countries, with each bar representing 100% of respondents for that nation, segmented by color into five categories: "Mostly help" (blue), "Neither" (green), "No response" (purple), "No opinion or don't know" (tan), and "Mostly harm" (red). In Japan and China, the blue segment is largest, with 61% of respondents in both nations expecting AI to mostly help. Germany follows closely at 60%, while Italy stands at 50%. Moving down the list, the United Kingdom shows 47% in the "Mostly help" category, and Canada and France both register 40%. In the United States and India, the blue segment shrinks to 35% and 33% respectively. Indonesia records 22% for "Mostly help," and Pakistan shows the lowest share in this category at 19%. Conversely, the red segment representing "Mostly harm" is smallest in Japan and China at 13% each, and largest in Pakistan at 52%. Indonesia and India both show 36% expecting harm, as does France. The tan segment for "No opinion or don't know" is notably large in Indonesia at 44% and in Canada and India at 30% each. The green "Neither" category remains small across most nations, ranging from 5% in Japan and France to 10% in Germany. The distribution shows a clear gradient where expectations of AI helping society decrease from East Asia and Europe toward South Asia, while expectations of harm increase along the same axis.
